# Feature: mcp-server

> Requires: schema_spec_format
> Scope: scripts/mcp/purlin_server.py, scripts/mcp/manifest.json
> Stack: python/stdlib, json, sys (JSON-RPC stdio transport)

## What it does

MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes Purlin tools to Claude Code via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio. Reads requests from stdin, dispatches to tool handlers (sync_status, purlin_config, changelog), and writes responses to stdout. Uses Python stdlib only — no external dependencies. The server auto-discovers the project root and loads specs/proofs from the filesystem.

## Rules

- RULE-1: The server implements JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol with `initialize`, `notifications/initialized`, `tools/list`, and `tools/call` methods
- RULE-2: `initialize` returns protocol version `2024-11-05`, server name `purlin`, and capabilities `{"tools": {}}`
- RULE-3: `tools/list` returns exactly 3 tools: `sync_status`, `purlin_config`, `changelog`
- RULE-4: `tools/call` dispatches to the correct handler based on the `name` parameter
- RULE-5: Unknown tool names return a JSON-RPC error with code `-32601`
- RULE-6: Unknown methods return a JSON-RPC error with code `-32601` (only if request has an `id`)
- RULE-7: Malformed JSON input returns a parse error with code `-32700`
- RULE-8: Notifications (no `id` field) receive no response
- RULE-9: Server startup message is written to stderr, not stdout

## Proof

- PROOF-1 (RULE-1): Send an `initialize` request; verify valid JSON-RPC response with `result`
- PROOF-2 (RULE-2): Send `initialize`; verify response contains `protocolVersion: "2024-11-05"` and `serverInfo.name: "purlin"`
- PROOF-3 (RULE-3): Send `tools/list`; verify response contains exactly 3 tools with correct names
- PROOF-4 (RULE-4): Send `tools/call` with `name: "sync_status"`; verify it returns a text content block
- PROOF-5 (RULE-5): Send `tools/call` with `name: "nonexistent"`; verify error code `-32601`
- PROOF-6 (RULE-6): Send an unknown method with an `id`; verify error response with code `-32601`
- PROOF-7 (RULE-7): Send malformed JSON; verify error response with code `-32700`
- PROOF-8 (RULE-8): Send `notifications/initialized` (no `id`); verify no response is written
- PROOF-9 (RULE-9): Start the server; verify startup message appears on stderr, not stdout

## Implementation Notes

- Design pattern: single-threaded stdio loop — read line, parse JSON, dispatch, write response (scripts/mcp/purlin_server.py:785-806)
- Key tradeoff: stdlib-only constraint means no async/framework overhead, but limits to synchronous processing
